article_title: Antibiotic Pipeline Shrinks 35 Percent in Five Years as WHO Races to Steer Development Toward Priority Superbugs

content_preview.title: Antibiotic Pipeline Shrinks 35 Percent in Five Years as WHO Races to Steer Development Toward Priority Superbugs
content_preview.summary: The 2026 AMR Benchmark reveals a 35 percent contraction in pharmaceutical antibiotic pipelines since 2021, while the WHO publishes new target product profiles for urgently needed drugs against carbapenem-resistant bacteria, vancomycin-resistant Enterococcus, and drug-resistant meningitis.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The global antibiotic pipeline is contracting at the very moment drug-resistant infections are accelerating. A [benchmark report](https://www.euronews.com/health/2026/03/11/antimicrobial-resistance-is-outpacing-the-industrys-efforts-to-fight-it-report-finds) published on March 11 by the Access to Medicine Foundation found that large research-based pharmaceutical companies now have just 60 antibiotic development projects, down 35 percent from 92 in 2021. On the same day, the [World H...

editor_notes:
Strong Analysis piece covering the antibiotic pipeline crisis with well-structured argument connecting market failure to public health outcomes. The loaded language warning ("clearly") is contextually appropriate — used in 'the science points clearly to what is needed' which is a reasonable editorial observation in an Analysis piece, not sensationalism. Summary accurately reflects content. Word count (1118) appropriate for Analysis category.

source_verification:
- euronews.com: CONFIRMED. Corroborates 60 projects down 35% from 92 in 2021, 15 companies assessed, GSK leads with 30 products, Shionogi and Pfizer, 7 late-stage projects, 39 million projected deaths 2025-2050, 35,000 EU deaths annually, Jayasree K. Iyer quote.
- who.int: CONFIRMED. Three TPPs for carbapenem-resistant gram-negatives, vancomycin-resistant Enterococcus faecium, and bacterial meningitis. EU4Health programme partnership. Dr. Yvan Hutin quote confirmed verbatim.
- statnews.com: CONFIRMED. AI cannot solve antibiotic market failure, structural changes needed for procurement/financing/regulation, AMR Action Fund involvement.
- frontiersin.org: CONFIRMED. Review of WHO-priority pathogens, integrated approaches spanning molecular biology to public health, antimicrobial stewardship, surveillance, One Health frameworks.
- nature.com: HTTP 303 redirect. Listed as background source but not directly cited in article body. Not critical to claim verification.
